title: Box breathing, equal sides
subtitle: A structured breathing meditation using the four-count box pattern to regulate the nervous system and slow the transition to sleep.
excerpt: A short breathing meditation for Day 9 of the SLEEP program. Equal sides: four counts in, four counts hold, four counts out, four counts hold. The symmetry is calming in itself.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 9 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025). Box breathing (also called square breathing) is used in military, clinical, and performance contexts for rapid nervous system regulation.

content.text: A short breathing meditation for Day 9 of the SLEEP program. Box breathing uses equal-length phases (inhale, hold, exhale, hold) to regulate the autonomic nervous system. The symmetry gives the mind something measurable to focus on, which interrupts the anxiety loop while the breath does its work.
Sit or lie comfortably. Let your breath settle for a moment before you begin counting.
Inhale through your nose for 4 counts.

If four counts per side feels too easy, extend to five or six, the count should be slow enough that each phase takes real time. If the holds feel uncomfortable, reduce to three counts and build from there.

Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 9 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025). Box breathing is used in military, clinical, and performance contexts for rapid nervous system regulation.
